$comment: Canonical source: a7p-cross/schema/a7p.schema.json. Copied here for standalone packaging (pip install) until the a7p-cross monorepo migration removes the need for a copy.
title: A7P Payload
description: Canonical schema for the .a7p profile format (protobuf Payload, snake_case as serialized on disk via MessageToDict(preserving_proto_field_name=True)). Numeric ranges are raw wire-format integers, i.e. the display-unit ranges from the original profile.clj (tvt.a7.profedit.profile) already multiplied by 10^x-fraction-digits. Fields tagged x-discrepancy document places where a7p-py / a7p-js / a7p-dart currently disagree with the value encoded here or with each other -- see DESIGN-schema-unification.md, open question #1, before treating this file as final.

bc_cd.description: Holds 'bc' (drag model G1/G7) or 'cd' (CUSTOM drag model); both share the same 0.0-10.0 display range.
bc_cd.x-fixed: a7p-js validate.ts capped this at raw 10000 (display max 1.0) instead of 100000 (display max 10.0), matching profile.clj / a7p-py / a7p-dart -- fixed.

mv.description: Muzzle velocity for G1/G7 rows (0.0-3000.0 m/s, 1 fraction digit -> raw max 30000), or Mach number 'ma' for CUSTOM rows (0.0-10.0, 4 fraction digits -> raw max 100000). Effective maximum is set contextually by the CoefRows if/then below.
mv.x-fixed: a7p-js capped CUSTOM mv at raw 10000 instead of 100000 -- same missing-factor-of-10 issue as bc_cd above -- fixed.

c_idx.x-discrepancy: a7p-js and a7p-dart's A7pValidator both accept the full 0-255 range unconditionally, i.e. they don't reject the undefined 201-254 gap that profile.clj / a7p-py exclude via oneOf. Confirm whether 201-254 should be rejected or the gap-exclusion should be dropped everywhere.

distance.description: Either the 'unused' sentinel 0, or a real distance in [100, 300000] raw (1.0-3000.0 m, 2 fraction digits) -- per profile.clj's (s/or :distance ::distance :unused zero?), unconditional on distance_from.
distance.x-discrepancy: a7p-js and a7p-dart instead branch on distance_from: when INDEX, they validate 'distance' as an index in [0,255] into the distances array; when VALUE, as [100,300000]. a7p-py/profile.clj do not make this distinction. This is a semantic fork, not just a range typo -- needs an explicit decision (see DESIGN doc open question) before encoding it here as an if/then on distance_from.

zoom.description: 0 = unset/default (observed in real profiles for switches with distance_from=INDEX acting as a placeholder entry), 1-6 = real zoom levels on the device.
zoom.x-fixed: a7p-dart's A7pValidator already checked 0-6 (matching this schema); its A7pFieldConstraints.zoom metadata said min 1, contradicting its own validator and the real shipped profiles below -- doc comment updated to 0-6.
zoom.x-discrepancy: profile.clj says 0-4 (documented bug, not used here). a7p-js validate.ts does not restrict this at all (0-255) -- not fixed yet. a7p-py's yupy_schema.py still hard-codes 0-4 -- narrower than canonical 0-6, meaning a legitimate zoom==5 or zoom==6 profile would currently be wrongly rejected by a7p-py (no real profile in the 484-file corpus currently hits this, so it hasn't surfaced as a test failure, but it's a latent bug). Confirmed against real shipped profiles: a7p-py/tests/data/example2.a7p and a7p-js/scripts/example2.a7p both ship a switch with zoom == 0.

c_muzzle_velocity.x-fixed: a7p-dart's A7pValidator checked minimum 10 instead of 100 (its own A7pFieldConstraints.muzzleVelocity implies raw min 100: 10.0 * 10^1) -- fixed.

c_zero_distance_idx.x-discrepancy: a7p-dart's A7pValidator instead bounds this dynamically by len(distances)-1 rather than a fixed 255 -- a cross-field constraint plain JSON Schema can't express without a custom keyword/extension. profile.clj / a7p-py / a7p-js all use the static 0-255 range encoded here.

c_zero_w_pitch.x-fixed: a7p-dart's A7pValidator did not check this field at all (present in A7pFieldConstraints but missing from _validateProfile) -- check added.

b_weight.x-fixed: a7p-dart's A7pValidator checked minimum 1 instead of 10 (its own A7pFieldConstraints.bulletWeight implies raw min 10: 1.0 * 10^1) -- fixed.

b_length.x-fixed: a7p-dart's A7pValidator used min 0 instead of 10 -- fixed.
b_length.x-discrepancy: a7p-js validate.ts still uses min 1 instead of 10 (profile.clj / a7p-py / a7p-dart all use raw min 10 = 0.01 * 10^3) -- not fixed yet, out of the confirmed fix scope for this pass.

coef_rows.description: Not expressible in plain JSON Schema: all 'mv' values must be unique except that any number of rows may have mv == 0. Enforce with one small hand-written check per language, run against schema/fixtures/ to keep behavior in sync.
